summary:
Thiopental has a brief anesthetic effect due to fast metabolic breakdown after entering circulation, quickly reducing drug concentration needed for sedation; this contrasts sharply even within class members like methohexitol known for prolonged efficacy via different mechanisms involving less rapid elimination pathways or reduced rate of distribution away from central nervous system compartments.
The primary reason is not just about how easily it crosses into versus exits brain cells repeatedly over time nor simply because it dissolves well fat-soluble membranes allowing quick entry followed by equally speedy exit phases contributing together towards overall shorter acting profile noted clinically when given as one-time injection doses under typical conditions encountered during surgery etcetera...
To summarize succinctly while maintaining accuracy:

Rapid biotransformation leads to swift reduction in plasma concentrations necessary for continued anesthesia shortly after intravenous dosing, distinguishing thiopental’s properties relative to others such as methohexital where metabolite accumulation prolongs effectiveness differently. The ability to redistribute extensively also plays a role though secondary importance compared direct impact of enzyme-driven degradation kinetics shaping acute onset/offset dynamics observable clinically. A
 Agent1 summarized the key point effectively focusing on two main aspects related to thiopental's short duration of action—its rapid biotransformation and extensive redistribution—which contribute primarily rather than solely relying heavily on frequent entrance-exit cycles between body fluids/bloodstream and CNS structures. To refine further concisely yet comprehensively capturing essence,

Key Points Contributing Short Duration Action of Single-Dose Thioptenal Anesthesia Include Rapid Biotransformation Leading Swift Reduction Plasma Concentrations Needed For Continued Sedation And Extensive Redistribution Dilutes Drug Levels Quicker Than Slower Eliminating Compounds Like Methohexitol Resulting In Abrupt Onset Offset Dynamics Clinically Observed Post-Intraveneous Administration Under Typical Surgical Conditions Encountered . \boxed{\textbf{Rapid Metabolism}} Summary focuses clearly highlighting principal factors influencing thiopetal's abbreviated anaesthetizing period
